name: ChatVLSC Secure API Route and Tunnel Map

recommended_auth: Bearer token validated by Worker or upstream identity provider
Cloudflare Access service token for machine-to-machine clients when enabled
routes.secret_handling: Never pass keys in query strings; use Authorization headers or Worker secrets only.

recommended_auth: Cloudflare Access application with owner/admin policy
Entra ID role claim check inside Worker
routes.secret_handling: Admin tokens must be Worker secrets or Cloudflare Access service credentials; do not publish to Notion or GitHub.

recommended_auth: Cloudflare Access service token before broader client testing
routes.secret_handling: Do not store development MCP credentials in .mcp.json, Notion, or GitHub.

recommended_auth: Token exchange and callback actions must validate state and identity claims
routes.secret_handling: Only display consent text and public metadata; never render client secrets or API tokens.

recommended_auth: Identity provider callback must validate tenant, GUID, nonce, and redirect URI
routes.secret_handling: Do not embed API keys in browser-delivered JavaScript.

tunnels.name: Admin Tunnel
tunnels.recommended_use: Private admin or origin connectivity only. Do not put public Worker routes behind this tunnel.
tunnels.secret_handling: Tunnel tokens are installation secrets; store only in Cloudflare/cloudflared service config and rotate if exposed.

recommended_applications.name: ChatVLSC Admin API
recommended_applications.policy: Allow owner/admin identities and approved service tokens only.

recommended_applications.name: ChatVLSC Development MCP
recommended_applications.policy: Allow development MCP clients through service-token or explicit identity-provider policy.

recommended_applications.name: ChatVLSC Current MCP
recommended_applications.policy: Allow approved service-token clients only for machine-to-machine MCP access.

allowed_storage: Cloudflare account Secrets Store when a store is authorized and bound for Worker use
disallowed_storage: mixed login handoff captures such as ChatVLSC Token.txt
key_usage_policy.rotation: Rotate any Cloudflare, OpenAI, tunnel, or service token that appears in chat, Notion, GitHub, logs, or browser-delivered output.

optimization_decisions: Keep public UI/auth display routes on Workers, not Tunnel.
Use Tunnel only for private origins or Workers VPC-style access to private services.
Keep the public homepage route on the dedicated homepage Worker and the API/admin/MCP/auth prefixes on the user-role MCP Worker.
Use one normalized Worker route handler for /user_role/v1, /admin/v1, /v1, /auth/v1, and /user_role/auth prefixes.
Set assets.run_worker_first=true so API and MCP routes enter Worker code before static asset lookup; /assets/* remains served through the ASSETS binding.
Require Access or equivalent Worker-side bearer-token enforcement before treating admin and development MCP endpoints as protected.
Do not use mixed ChatVLSC Token handoff text for Microsoft Graph; require a clean Graph token with Application.ReadWrite.All.
Keep sanitized endpoint maps in GitHub and Notion; keep credentials only in secret stores.
Preserve request_limit_fail_open=false on all Worker routes.
